title: jasione crispa subsp. centralis
intro: Jasione crispa subsp. centralis, also known as central rockrose or mountain rockrose, is a subspecies of the Jasione crispa plant, a perennial herb native to the mountainous regions of Europe and North Africa. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which make it useful for wound healing and skin issues. The health benefits of central rockrose include its ability to reduce inflammation and alleviate respiratory problems, such as bronchitis and asthma, due to its expectorant properties. Central rockrose can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to treat various health conditions, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
